element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Slade joins during dungeon escape sequence; ongoing throughout game
Available at level 20; some characters have branching promotion paths
Require specific events or conditions to unlock and recruit
Core mechanic; turn-based with movement and action economy
Unlocked via character class and leveling
Customization via town headquarters or during battles
No chapter locks; can revisit areas; drives narrative progression

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Episodic tension with story beats punctuating tactical encounters; early game introduces mechanics through sequential threats (Gizmos → possession → invasion), mid-game shifts to open-world exploration with self-directed pacing, late-game escalates through puzzle-solving and ally gathering

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden characters tied to specific locations and event triggers; hidden treasures discoverable during exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Special hidden promotion items unlock branching class advancement paths; some allies require event fulfillment rather than story progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction phase: weak Gizmo enemies, tutorial-style combat with small squad; establishes grid-based mechanics and party-building foundation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation: possession arc and territorial conflict introduce stronger commanders and larger enemy formations; Parmecia migration marks shift to open-world exploration with self-directed difficulty scaling via optional encounters
difficulty_curve.late_game: Convergence: gather scattered allies, solve Jewel riddle, obtain Force Sword; culminates in sealed demon king confrontation with high enemy variety and stat requirements
notable_spikes: King Galam possession battle (first major commander fight)
Tower of Ancients collapse sequence (story-driven difficulty jump)

notable_level_patterns.description: Tower of Ancients → Galam dungeon → Granseal invasion: tight, story-driven sequence introducing combat and party formation with limited backtracking
Parmecia continent structure: multiple towns as safe zones, battles as optional encounters between them, free roaming enables exploration-driven progression and hidden character discovery
Large-scale tactical engagement against territorial or demonic forces; typically guards strategic location or possesses key objective (hostage, jewel)
Confined space where party must break out and recover allies; introduces NPC recruitment during hostile traversal
